title: Association v1
description: An abstraction derived from the corpus: what remains true when the particulars change. A Finding is historical (this combo did this); an Association is invariant (this holds across combos). Never formed from a single workflow — the evidence-log discipline in docs/future-capability-ontology-notes.md requires repeated conceptual convergence across distinct workflows. Derived, never authored: re-running the projection over the same events reproduces it byte-for-byte.

association_id.description: Deterministic identity derived from association_type + pattern + invariant, so re-projection updates the abstraction in place instead of duplicating it.

pattern.description: Which abstraction axis produced this association (e.g. task_backend: hold task_kind+backend constant, vary builder+model).

statement.description: The abstraction, stated for a human operator.

invariant.description: The fields that stayed constant while everything else changed — the content of the abstraction.

varied.description: The particulars that changed under the invariant: field -> distinct observed values. At least one field must have varied, or this is a repeated particular, not an abstraction.

workflows.description: The distinct workflows the invariant held across. minItems 2 IS the no-generalization-from-one-workflow rule, enforced at the contract.

confidence.description: Keyed to the number of distinct workflows, not raw samples: an abstraction earns confidence from independent contexts.

confidence_score.description: workflows/(workflows+2) — the standard evidence curve applied to context count. Never 1.0.

failure_class.description: failure_invariant only: the single failure class every member observation shares.

supporting_findings.description: finding_ids for the member combos — provenance down into the findings layer.
